title.undefined: MedDevOnt - European Medical Device Catalogue Ontology

description.undefined: An ontology modelling the European catalogue of high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices under MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746).

rights.value: This ontology is distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

abstract.value: MedDevOnt provides a formal semantic representation of the EUDAMED catalogue, covering medical devices, manufacturers, risks, incidents and field safety corrective actions

title.value: MedDevOnt - European Medical Device Catalogue Ontology

comment.undefined: Probability level indicating that the risk event is likely to occur under normal conditions of use.
An active device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body and to remain there after the procedure.
Regulation (EU) 2017/745 on medical devices, classifying devices into classes I, IIa, IIb and III.
Highest risk class under MDR (EU 2017/745). Devices are subject to the most stringent conformity assessment, including clinical evaluation.
Severity level indicating a risk that could result in death or permanent serious injury.
Risk category covering events related to intrinsic safety hazards of the device, including cybersecurity threats.
An action to remove or correct a distributed device that presents a risk to health or does not conform to regulations.
Highest risk class under IVDR (EU 2017/746). Devices pose the highest risk, such as tests for HIV or blood-group typing.
Medium-high risk class under IVDR (EU 2017/746). Devices require notified body intervention for conformity assessment.
The type of field safety corrective action taken by a manufacturer in response to a safety incident.
Lowest risk class under MDR (EU 2017/745). Devices pose minimal risk to patients and users.
Low-medium risk class under IVDR (EU 2017/746). Devices require limited notified body involvement.
Lowest risk class under IVDR (EU 2017/746). Devices pose minimal risk and include general laboratory instruments.

A legal entity responsible for the design, manufacture and placing on the market of a medical device, registered in EUDAMED.
Risk arising from exploitable software vulnerabilities enabling unauthorised access or manipulation of device functions.
The classification of a reported incident based on its actual or potential impact on patient safety.
An incident where a malfunction occurred but did not lead to patient harm, though it had the potential to do so.
Risk arising from the device failing to perform its intended function correctly.

A device that combines both active and implantable characteristics, requiring energy to 
operate while being implanted in the body.
Risk of incorrect diagnostic output from an in vitro diagnostic device, including false positive or false negative results.
A written communication sent to users providing updated instructions, warnings or procedural changes.
The specific type of risk mechanism associated with a hazard, following IMDRF risk terminology.
The likelihood of a risk event occurring during the expected lifetime of the device.
Rule - Devices classified under risk class III or class D are automatically considered high-risk devices.
Risk category covering events related to suboptimal or unintended device performance without complete failure.
Probability level indicating that the risk event is unlikely to occur under normal conditions of use.
Risk of reagent or device contamination leading to systematic errors or patient harm.
An incident that directly or indirectly led to death, serious deterioration in health, or serious public health threat.
Rule - Manufacturers linked to devices with incidents that have open FSCAs are classified as active FSCA manufacturers.
A communication issued to healthcare professionals warning of a safety risk and providing updated instructions.
Risk category covering events where an in vitro diagnostic device produces incorrect results leading to misdiagnosis.
Risk of direct physical harm to a patient or user caused by the device.
A correction performed on a device at the location where it is installed or in use, without requiring its removal.

A device intended to be used on one individual during a single procedure and then discarded.
The degree of harm that a risk event could cause to a patient, user or third party.

A medical device or in vitro diagnostic device registered in the EUDAMED catalogue under MDR or IVDR regulation.
The risk classification assigned to a medical device according to its applicable regulatory framework.
The European regulatory framework under which a medical device is classified and registered.
Regulation (EU) 2017/746 on in vitro diagnostic medical devices, classifying devices into classes A, B, C and D.
Probability level indicating that the risk event may occur occasionally under normal conditions of use.
Risk of electromagnetic or physical interference affecting device performance or diagnostic imaging quality.
A device whose operation depends on a source of energy other than that generated by the human body or gravity.
Risk of microbial contamination or device-related infection at the implantation site or systemically.
Status indicating that the field safety corrective action is currently active and not yet completed.
Risk of unintended ionising or non-ionising radiation exposure caused by the device.
A device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body and to remain there after the procedure, without depending on an energy source.
Rule - Serious incidents without corrective actions taken are classified as unresolved incidents.
Severity level indicating a risk that could result in serious but reversible injury or hospitalisation.
Risk arising from software defects causing incorrect device behaviour, dosing errors or alarm failures.
Risk category covering events where the device fails to perform its intended function due to electrical, mechanical or software issues.
Risk arising from adverse biological reactions to device materials, including wear debris or metallic ion release.
Risk category covering events related to contamination, sterility failures or degradation of device components.
The current status of a field safety corrective action, indicating whether it is still ongoing or has been completed.
The functional and structural classification of a medical device based on its mode of action and intended use.

A serious incident or near-miss event reported in relation to a specific medical device as required by MDR or IVDR.
A device intended for the examination of specimens derived from the human body to provide information on physiological or pathological conditions.
The high-level grouping of risks according to the nature of the hazard, based on IMDRF terminology.
Rule - Incidents linked to risks with critical severity are classified as critical incidents.
Risk category covering events that directly cause or may cause physical injury, infection or adverse reaction in a patient.
An in vitro diagnostic device intended to be used by lay persons in a home environment.
Medium-high risk class under MDR (EU 2017/745). Devices require more stringent conformity assessment procedures.
Medium-low risk class under MDR (EU 2017/745). Devices require involvement of a notified body for conformity assessment.
Status indicating that the field safety corrective action has been fully completed and verified.
Severity level indicating a risk that could result in minor injury or temporary harm not requiring hospitalisation.

A corrective action taken by a manufacturer to reduce the risk of death or serious deterioration in health associated with a medical device.
Relates a medical device to the European regulation under which it is classified.
The date on which the incident actually occurred.
Relates a device type to the medical device classified under it.
The intended use of the medical device as specified by the manufacturer in the technical documentation.
Indicates whether a field safety corrective action was initiated in response to this incident.
A description of the scope of the field safety corrective action, including affected lot numbers or device ranges.
Relates a risk to the type of device it typically affects according to IMDRF classification.
The unique reference number assigned to the incident report in the EUDAMED database.
Relates an incident to the manufacturer that submitted the report to the competent authority.
A detailed description of the field safety corrective action taken and its expected outcome.
The registered legal name of the manufacturer as recorded in EUDAMED.
Relates a medical device to its functional and structural device type classification.
Relates a risk to its severity level indicating the degree of potential harm.
The commercial name of the medical device as registered in EUDAMED.
Relates a field safety corrective action to the incident that caused it.
The number or description of devices affected by the field safety corrective action.
Relates a risk to the incident it is associated with.
The list of countries in which the field safety corrective action has been implemented.
A detailed narrative description of the incident, including the device involved and the clinical context.
Relates an incident to the medical device it was reported against.
The human-readable name of the risk as defined in the IMDRF nomenclature.
A description of the clinical outcome or consequence of the incident for the patient or user.
Indicates whether the device is classified as an in vitro diagnostic device under IVDR.
Relates an action type to the FSCA it classifies.
The unique reference number assigned to the field safety corrective action in the EUDAMED database.
The EU member state in which the incident was reported to the competent authority.
Relates a device type to the risk that affects it.
The alphanumeric code assigned to the risk according to the IMDRF terminology for adverse event coding.
Relates a medical device to its regulatory risk classification under MDR or IVDR.
The date on which the incident or device malfunction was first detected.
The date on which the field safety corrective action was completed and verified.
The unique single registration number assigned to the manufacturer upon registration in EUDAMED.
Relates an incident to the field safety corrective action issued in response to it.
Relates a risk to its specific IMDRF risk mechanism type.
Relates a severity level to the risk it describes.
Relates a risk category to the risk it classifies.
Indicates whether the device is intended to be totally or partially implanted in the human body.
Relates a field safety corrective action to its type of intervention.
The date on which the field safety corrective action was initiated by the manufacturer.
The age in years of the patient involved in the incident at the time of the event.
Relates an incident type to the incident it classifies.
The national regulatory authority responsible for supervising the manufacturer.
Relates a risk to its high-level IMDRF hazard category.
The country where the manufacturer's registered office is located.
Relates a medical device to its registered manufacturer in EUDAMED.
The unique device identifier at device level assigned to a medical device under MDR or IVDR.
Relates a regulatory framework to the medical device regulated under it.
Relates a risk to its probability level indicating the likelihood of occurrence.
Relates a manufacturer to an incident it has reported to the competent authority.
The national authorization number assigned to the manufacturer by the competent authority.
Relates a manufacturer to a medical device it is responsible for.
Relates a probability level to the risk it describes.
Indicates whether the device is intended to be used on more than one individual or procedure.
Relates a risk class to the medical device it is assigned to.
The biological sex of the patient involved in the incident.
Relates a risk type to the risk it characterises.
Relates a status to the FSCA it describes.
Indicates whether the device requires an external energy source to operate.
Relates an incident to its classification as serious or near-miss.
Relates an incident to the IMDRF risk associated with the reported event.
Relates a field safety corrective action to its current open or closed status.
Relates a medical device to a reported serious incident or near-miss event.
The date on which the incident was formally reported to the competent authority.
The official regulatory contact email address of the manufacturer.
A detailed description of the risk mechanism and its potential consequences for patients or users.
